claim: Paris is the capital of France
evidence: Paris has been the capital of France since the 12th century and is the seat of the French government.

claim: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye
evidence: NASA has confirmed that the Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye. This is a common myth.

claim: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level
evidence: At standard atmospheric pressure (1 atmosphere), water boils at exactly 100°C (212°F).
William Shakespeare is credited with writing 37 plays, though some scholars debate the exact number.

claim: The human brain uses 50% of the body's energy
evidence: The human brain uses approximately 20% of the body's total energy, not 50%. This is a common misconception.

claim: Mount Everest is the tallest mountain on Earth
evidence: Mount Everest stands at 8,848.86 meters (29,031.7 feet) above sea level, making it the tallest mountain on Earth.
Scientific studies have shown goldfish can remember things for months, not just 3 seconds. This is a persistent myth.

claim: The Declaration of Independence was signed in 1776
evidence: The Declaration of Independence was approved by the Continental Congress on July 4, 1776.

claim: Bananas are berries, but strawberries are not
evidence: Botanically speaking, bananas are classified as berries because they develop from a single flower with one ovary. Strawberries are not true berries.

claim: Lightning never strikes the same place twice
evidence: Lightning frequently strikes the same place multiple times. Tall buildings and structures are hit repeatedly.

claim: The Amazon rainforest produces 20% of the world's oxygen
evidence: The Amazon rainforest is often called the 'lungs of the Earth' and produces approximately 20% of the world's oxygen.

claim: Penguins live at the North Pole
evidence: Penguins live in the Southern Hemisphere, primarily in Antarctica. Polar bears live at the North Pole.

claim: The first iPhone was released in 2007
evidence: Apple released the first iPhone on June 29, 2007, revolutionizing the smartphone industry.
Extensive scientific research has conclusively shown no link between vaccines and autism. This claim has been thoroughly debunked.

claim: There are more possible games of chess than atoms in the observable universe
evidence: The number of possible chess games (Shannon number) is estimated at 10^120, while there are approximately 10^80 atoms in the observable universe.
